title: ranunculus sceleratus var. sceleratus
intro: Ranunculus sceleratus var. sceleratus, also known as the Cursed Buttercup, is a type of flowering plant in the buttercup family, characterized by its bright yellow or orange flowers and toxic sap. Traditionally, the plant's roots, stems, and leaves have been used in herbal medicine, particularly for their anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Ranunculus sceleratus var. sceleratus include the treatment of various skin conditions, such as eczema and acne, as well as its ability to reduce fever and alleviate pain. Herbal preparations of the plant may include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which can be used externally or internally to take advantage of its medicinal properties.
